data.title: Oracle plans to spend $40B on Nvidia chips to power OpenAI data center: report
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) plans to spend about $40B on Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) high-end chips to power OpenAI's new Texas data center, according to a report by the Financial Times.

The data center will be the first constructed as part of the $500B U.S. Stargate Project, according to the report. The 1.2 GW data center will be located in Abilene, Texas, and will be one of the largest in the world. Oracle will purchase 400,000 of Nvidia's GB200 chips for training and running artificial intelligence applications. The new data center is expected to start operations by the middle of 2026. 

The Stargate Project was officially announced in January by U.S. President Donald Trump, along with SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY), Oracle and OpenAI. Arm (ARM), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Nvidia were also named as key partners in the project. SoftBank and OpenAI have each contributed $18B to Stargate. 

Earlier this week, it was announced that OpenAI, Nvidia, Oracle, SoftBank, Cisco Systems (CSCO) and G42 are collaborating to build Stargate UAE. The 1-GW compute cluster will be built by G42 and operated by OpenAI and Oracle. That project will also utilize Nvidia's Grace Blackwell chips.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on Nvidia, OPEC, and box office blockbusters
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - May 26

  • All day - The U.S. stock market is closed for observance of the Memorial Day holiday.
  • All day - The U.S. box office numbers for the extended four-day holiday weekend will be reported. Disney's (DIS) live-action Lilo & Stitch is expected to bring in between $185 million to $200 million, while Paramount's (PARA) Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning is forecast to see a tally in the $75 million and $85 million range.

 

Tuesday - May 27

  • Volatility watch - Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) and UroGen Pharma (URGN) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include NervGen Pharma (OTCQB:NGENF), SatixFy Communications (SATX), and Regulus Therapeutics (RGLS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Spirit Aviation (FLYY), Compass Diversified (CODI), and B&G Foods (BGS).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include AutoZone (AZO), Okta (OKTA), and Box (BOX).
  • Fed watch - Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari speaks and participates in a policy panel before the Bank of Japan Institute for Monetary and Economic Studies Conference. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will participate in a moderated discussion panel before the Bank of Japan Institute for Monetary and Economic Studies Conference.
  • All day - The three-day Bitcoin 2025 Conference will feature talks by Vice President JD Vance, Strategy (MSTR) CEO Michael Saylor, and Robinhood Markets (HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev.
  • All day - PENN Entertainment (PENN), Oklo (OKLO), and DraftKings (DKNG) are some of the notable companies due to present at the Craig-Hallum Institutional Investor Conference.
  • All day - Aya Gold & Silver (AYA:CA) (OTCQX:AYASF) will present at the Raymond James Silver Day.

 

Wednesday - May 28

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nvidia (NVDA), Salesforce (CRM), Bank of Montreal (BMO), Agilent Technologies (A), HP (HPQ), Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), and Pure Storage (PSTG). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for e.l.f. Beauty (ELF) and C3.ai (AI) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Zentalis Pharmaceuticals (ZNTL), Aura Biosciences (AURA), and Protara Therapeutics (TARA) are some of the companies due to present at the TD Cowen's Oncology Innovation Summit.
  • All day - The 60th session of the Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee of OPEC and non-OPEC countries is scheduled to take place in Vienna, Austria. The committee will pay particular attention to the additional voluntary production adjustments made by eight OPEC+ countries, which were announced in April and November 2023, and are intended to support market stability.
  • All day - Other notable investor events include Coherent Corp.'s (COHR) Investor Day, Performance Food Group's (PFGC) Investor Day, Exxon Mobil's (XOM) annual meeting, First Advantage's (FA) Investor Day, and Chevron's (CVX) annual meeting.
  • All day - Horizon Aircraft (HOVR), Honeywell (HON), Blade Air Mobility (BLDE), and Surf Air Mobility (SRFM) are some of the companies confirmed to be participating in the Jefferies eVTOL Summit.
  • 8:30 a.m. AMD (AMD) will present at the TD Cowen Technology, Media and Telecom Conference.
  • 8:30 a.m. Visa (V) will present at the Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from the last meeting of the FOMC.

 

Thursday - May 29

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Costco Wholesale (COST), Royal Bank of Canada (RY), Dell Technologies (DELL), Marvell Technology (MRVL), Zscaler (ZS), and Li Auto (LI). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Caleres (CAL), Pagerduty (PD), and Starz (STRZ) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Ashland (ASH) will hold its Innovation Day for analysts and investors at the company’s Bridgewater, New Jersey facility. The event will showcase the enhanced capabilities and expanded applications of Ashland's scalable technology platforms.
  • 10:40 a.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee participates in a moderated question-and-answer session before the 2025 Mackinac Policy Conference hosted by the Detroit Regional Chamber.
  • 4:00 p.m. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will participate in a fireside chat before the Oakland Rotary Club.

 

Friday - May 30

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Shoe Carnival (SCVL).
  • All day - The five-day ASCO Conference will begin. The major oncology conference will include a flurry of abstracts and poster presentations that could impact share prices in the healthcare sector.
  • All day - EA Sports F1 25 will be released by Electronic Arts (EA) for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.
  • All day - The Ira Sohn Conference Hong Kong will take place. The conference has led to share price jumps for some stocks in the past after hedge funds made pitches.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for April will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.6% year-over-year in March.
  • 7:30 p.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated Q&A before "The Interview Show with Mark Bazer."

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Trump signs executive orders aiming to expand nuclear power, speed up reactor construction
data.description:
President Trump signed executive orders Friday aimed at expanding the production of nuclear energy and easing the regulatory process on approvals for new nuclear reactors.

Stocks related to nuclear power and uranium production have been surging after Reuters reported late Thursday that the executive orders were coming: Lightbridge (LTBR) +43%, Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE) +31%, Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) +28.1%, Uranium Energy (UEC) +25.5%, Centrus Energy (LEU) +24.5%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) +19.6%, NuScale Power (SMR) +18.3%, NexGen Energy (NXE) +14.7%, ASP Isotopes (ASPI) +13.6%, Denison Mines (DNN) +11.3%, Cameco (CCJ) +10%, BWX Technologies (BWXT) +9.9%, Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) +2.1%, Vistra (VST) +2.1%, Talen Energy (TLN) +1.7%, NRG Energy (NRG) +1.6%.

Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) CEO Joseph Dominguez and Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) CEO Jacob DeWitte accompanied Trump and spoke at the signing.

The orders are intended to reform nuclear energy research at the U.S. Department of Energy, clear a path to allow the DoE to build nuclear reactors on federally-owned land, overhaul the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, and expand uranium mining and enrichment in the U.S.

ETFs: (URA), (NLR), (URNM)

data.title: Newmont continues rally for seventh session in a row
data.description:
Newmont (NYSE:NEM) was extending gains for the seventh straight session on Friday as its shares added 1.73% to reach $53.56 during afternoon trading.

The gold mining firm’s shares rose nearly 6% in the past six trading sessions. It closed almost flat at $52.65 on Thursday. It began its upward trajectory on May 15 after declining in the previous three sessions following the US-China trade deal. The stock was one of the five largest losers on the S&P 500 following the deal which dented gold’s appeal as a safe-haven asset.

Shortly after, the company was downgraded by Seeking Alpha analyst JR Research from Buy to Hold. They argued that Newmont has lagged behind the VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) since July 2022, despite being the top constituent and benefiting from rising gold prices.

“Newmont's FCF margins are projected to stay strong, but elevated AISC and production challenges may hinder near-term performance,” the analyst added.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, NEM has been rated a Strong Buy with a score of 4.81 out of 5 with an A+ for profitability and B+ for momentum. Wall Street as well as Seeking Alpha analysts issued a Buy rating for the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Eliana Scialabba believes that Newmont's valuation multiples are appealing, presenting a strong risk-reward opportunity for long-term investors.

“Newmont undervalues its real value when measured against what it does best: consistently generate cash, operate with margins well above the industry average and maintain a strong financial structure. When I compare its multipliers linked to the actual profits with those of other mining companies, I consider that its price is more than reasonable for the quality of the business it offers,” she said, rating it a Buy.

data.title: Global cancer drug sales growth to slow amid patent cliffs: report
data.description:
Spending on cancer drugs is expected to reach $441B globally in 2029 despite a slowdown later in the decade as key oncology medicines lose market exclusivity, drug analytics and contract research firm IQVIA (IQV) said in a recent report.

In this year’s edition of its global oncology trends report, IQV’s data analytics unit IQVIA Institute added that spending on cancer medicines expanded 75% over the past five years to reach $252B in 2024.

After averaging 11.9% growth annually from 2020 to 2024, the spending growth is expected to slow from 2027 as some of the key oncology products lose market exclusivity, paving the way for generic and biosimilar competition.

Notable cancer drugs facing patent cliffs include Ibrance from Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and Xtandi from Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/Astellas (OTCPK:ALPMF) (OTCPK:ALPMY). Both small molecule drugs lose market exclusivity in 2027.

Meanwhile, PD-1 inhibitors, Keytruda from Merck (NYSE:MRK) and Opdivo from Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY), which made up a tenth of global cancer spending in 2024 will no longer enjoy patent protection from 2028 largely impacting oncology spending growth in 2029, IQV said.

However, antibody-drug conjugates, bispecific antibodies, and cell/gene therapies will offset the impact, contributing to roughly 20% of oncology expenditure in 2029, up from 9% and 3% in 2024 and 2019, respectively.

The report noted that major developed markets, led by the U.S., which made up 74% of cancer drug spending in 2024, are expected to witness a slowdown in growth over the next five years, especially in 2028 and 2029, amid generic and biosimilar competition.

In terms of R&D trends, Iqvia (IQV) said that Chinese companies have led 39% of newly started oncology trials in 2024, up from only 5% a decade ago, with 84% of their trials concentrated locally.

“Trials run by China-headquartered companies have seen significant growth in the last decade, highlighting the important role that companies headquartered there will play in the development of new products globally,” the firm said.

Meanwhile, the U.S. has accounted for 32% of oncology trial starts in 2024, down 5% since 2019, and Europe’s share in new cancer trials has dropped 14% over the past five years to 20%.

Leading cancer drugmakers: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), Merck (NYSE:MRK), Bristol-Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY), Eli Lilly (LLY), AstraZeneca (AZN), GSK (GSK), Novartis (NVS) (OTCPK:NVSEF), AbbVie (ABBV), Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) (OTCQX:RHHBY), Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ), Amgen (AMGN)

data.title: Apple, Samsung tariffs coming by end of June, Trump says
data.description:
• Tariffs on all smartphones not built in the U.S., notably those from Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), are coming by the end of June, President Trump said on Friday.
  • Speaking to reporters outside the White House, Trump reiterated his stance that Apple can build iPhones in the U.S. — “It's computerized,” Trump said, perhaps a reference to iPhone's manufacturing plants. However, Trump added that if Apple and Samsung were to build plants in the U.S., there would be no tariffs.
  • Apple shares fell 2.7% on Friday, extending their losses from earlier in the session, after President Trump said that Apple would be hit with a 25% tariff on iPhones that are sold in the U.S. and not made domestically. 
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wall Street pares losses as traders digest Trump's tariff threats, Bessent's comments
data.description:
Wall Street recovered some of its decline on Friday, after remarks from U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent calmed investors rattled by President Donald Trump's latest salvo of tariff threats. 

Bessent in an interview said "several, large" trade deals could be announced soon. Trump in the morning had threatened to impose tariffs on tech giant Apple (AAPL) and the European Union (EU).

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) was last -0.25% at 5,827.42 points in late afternoon trade, having fallen as much as -1.28%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.17% at 41,785.89 points, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.49% at 18,832.12 points.

"We have 18 important trading partners, so what everyone should really focus on are those. We've done a deal with the UK. My sense is, over the next couple of weeks, we're going to have several, large deals announced...I expect that we will be negotiating in person with (China) again," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a Bloomberg TV interview.

Earlier, the impact of Trump's announcements on his Truth Social service was immediate, with stock index futures plunging. 

First, Trump took aim at Apple (AAPL). He pitched a tariff of at least 25% on the tech giant's products if it did not build its iPhones in the U.S. Apple (AAPL) shares were last -2.6%. 

Then, Trump targeted the EU. After pointing out several of the EU's "unfair" practices, he asserted that trade discussions with the union were "going nowhere" and that he was recommending a straight 50% tariff on EU goods starting June 1.   

The EU is one of the biggest trading partners of the United States. According to the U.S. Trade Representative, total goods trade with the European Union were an estimated $975.9B in 2024.

Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning, advised market participants that the EU tariff recommendation should not be taken at "face value."

"Goes without saying but no one should take any of these hyperbolic statements at face value as we've learned again and again over the past few months. There is no scenario where 50% tariffs on the EU would last for very long because the markets would revolt and a deal to lower them would quickly be reached," Bilello said on X.    

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields continued to stabilize following a bruising bond sell-off on Wednesday. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 2 basis points at 4.52%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was little changed at 4.00%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: Bitcoin to rise on weekly basis, touches new high in the period
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to rise 5.6% on a weekly basis as it scaled a new high in the period but fell on Friday after President Donald Trump threatened tariffs on Apple (AAPL) and the European Union.

The world’s largest digital cryptocurrency started off the week above the $105K mark and continued to climb up over the remaining days.

“We anticipate more volatility once Bitcoin breaks above $110k, entering uncharted price discovery territory, while unstable geopolitical and macroeconomic factors lead investors to consider the long-term value of Bitcoin,” said Jupiter Zheng, Partner, Liquid Fund and Research, HashKey Capital.

Fueled by institutional ETF inflows, corporate Treasury adoption, and its evolving role in macroeconomic hedging, Bitcoin's swift rebounds and demand above $100k underscore its maturation as a strategic asset, Zheng added.

Trump, through his social media platform Truth Social, said he is recommending a straight 50% tariff on the European Union, commencing on June 1, 2025.

Trump said Apple (AAPL) would have to pay 25% tariffs on iPhones not made in the U.S.

Bitcoin on Wednesday breached the $110K mark as supportive regulatory developments in the U.S. have added momentum to the market, reinforcing the credibility of crypto as a legitimate investment class.

Notably, congressional lawmakers are nearing consensus on legislation that would establish a regulatory framework for the $250B stablecoin market.

“The correlation between Bitcoin and equities has evolved from non-correlated to a positive correlation since 2020,” Seeking Alpha analyst CME group said.

The heightened positive correlation during market volatility implies that bitcoin is exhibiting equity-like behavior. Higher positive correlations are frequently observed during periods of market stress, CME Group added.

The cryptocurrency’s surge came on the same day as Wall Street slid, amid a bruising bond sell-off sparked by concerns over U.S. fiscal debt, with a weak Treasury auction adding to the gloom.

On Thursday, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) forged to a new high on the 15th anniversary of the first commercial transaction that used the world's first cryptocurrency.

“Bullish momentum is reinforced by weakness in the U.S. Dollar Index and limited upside in the S&P 500, favoring capital rotation into cryptocurrencies,” Seeking Alpha analyst Income Generator said.

I will reconsider my outlook if Bitcoin falls below $106,140, but current sentiment and technicals support further upside in the near term, Income Generator added.

“We think that large institutions are driving Bitcoin's rally as more and more publicly listed companies such as Strategy and Metaplanet continue to raise capital and invest in Bitcoin. Investors are also crowding into Bitcoin ETFs, which saw $3.6bn in net inflows in May,” said Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE.

We believe this trend will continue as long as companies continue to tap public markets for more capital. This could even accelerate if the Fed decides to cut interest rates in the coming months, Mei added.

Notable News

Top U.S. banks are looking into potentially teaming up to issue a joint stablecoin, The Wall Street Journal has reported, signaling that traditional and cryptocurrency finance may be moving closer together.

Crypto exchange Kraken is planning to start offering tokenized versions of Apple (AAPL), Tesla (TSLA), Nvidia (NVDA) and other high-profile stocks for its non-U.S. customers, according to a media report dated Thursday, a move that would further blur the line between traditional finance and digital assets.

Basel Medical (BMGL) entered into a purchase agreement with a consortium of Bitcoin holders for the purchase of up to 10,000 BTC with current market value of ~$1B by issuing its ordinary shares.

Coinbase (COIN) recent data breach had compromised 69,461 of the crypto exchange's users, according to a complaint filed with the Main Attorney General's office. About 217 of that total were Maine residents.

Strategy (MSTR) announced a sales agreement to issue shares of its 10.00% Series A Perpetual Strife Preferred Stock (STRF) worth up to $2.1B in an at-the-market offering.

Riot Platforms (RIOT) has upsized its existing $100M credit facility to a total commitment of up to $200M with Coinbase Credit, a subsidiary of Coinbase.

Circle Internet Financial (CRCL), the issuer of stablecoin USDC (USDC-USD), refuted on Monday a media report that the company was in talks for a potential sale.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) said in an SEC filing on Friday that CFO Jason Warnick sold shares worth about $3.1M in the stock and crypto trading app.

Crypto Moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell 1.9% to $109.3K as of 2:14 pm ET on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) was down 2.5% to $2.6K. Doge (DOGE-USD) was 2.2% lower.

data.title: Arbor Realty Trust CEO adds stake worth ~$2M, shares up 6%
data.description:
Arbor Realty Trust (NYSE:ABR) said in an SEC filing that CEO Ivan Kaufman added stake worth more than $1.9M in the mortgage REIT.

Kaufman purchased 210,000 shares of common stock in multiple transactions at prices ranging from $8.45 to $9.91 per share.

ABR was trading 6.05% higher to $9.46 Friday afternoon even as Wall Street was down, with sentiment slammed by U.S. President Donald Trump's threats to impose tariffs on tech giant Apple and the European Union.

data.title: e.l.f. Beauty raising prices $1 on inflation, import tariffs
data.description:
• E.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) is raising the price of its cosmetics by $1 beginning August 1 to compensate for inflation and import tariffs.
  • “Bringing you the best of beauty is getting more $$$ but we’re committed to keeping the quality high and prices e.l.f.fordable,” the company said on Instagram (META).
  • Nearly 80% of e.l.f. Beauty’s (NYSE:ELF) products are made in China, with the remainder sourced from Hong Kong, South Korea, and Poland.
  • By raising prices, e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) risks further alienating customers as the company recently lowered its outlook for net sales growth, citing softer consumption trends, challenging category conditions, and slower-than-expected new product performance.
  • E.l.f. Beauty (ELF) joins other retailers who have confirmed price hikes, including Nike (NKE), Walmart (WMT), Mattel (MAT), and Best Buy (BBY).
